channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: Priorities for advancing artificial intelligence in inflammatory bowel disease: a technology update.
item.description: Gastrointestinal endoscopy - Review - Artificial intelligence (AI), encompassing various methods that enable computers to process complex data and perform tasks previously requiring human input, is set to play a pivotal role in the fut...

item.title: Multimodal deep learning for inflammatory bowel disease: a new frontier in cellular and molecular biomarker discovery to clinical translation.
item.description: Journal of biological engineering - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), including Crohn’s disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), is a chronic, relapsing condition with heterogeneous clinical phenotypes and variable therapeutic outco...

item.title: Artificial intelligence in inflammatory bowel disease: bridging innovation, implementation and impact.
item.description: Nature reviews. Gastroenterology & hepatology - Review - Artificial intelligence (AI) is rapidly transforming the management landscape of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). While early applications in endoscopy, digital pathology and cross-sectional imagi...

item.title: The roles and heterogeneity of CD8+ T cells in inflammatory bowel disease: A narrative review of insights from single‑cell transcriptomics (Review).
item.description: International journal of molecular medicine - Review - The present review investigates the role and characteristics of CD8+ T cells in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) using single‑cell transcriptomics, revealing their pivotal functions and remarkable ...

item.title: T cell and cytokine signatures as early predictors of response to IL-12/IL-23 inhibition in Crohn's disease.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Crohn's disease (CD) is a disabling inflammatory disorder with highly variable response to biologic therapies. Despite the availability of multiple biologic agents, a significant proportion of pati...

item.title: Longitudinal Safety Outcomes of Anti-tumor Necrosis Factor Alpha Therapy in the Treatment of Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Short- and medium-term safety profiles of anti-TNF therapies are well-established, but data on their long-term safety, especially beyond five years, remain limited. This study aims to compare the r...

item.title: Positive conversion of latent tuberculosis screening in patients with inflammatory bowel disease on antitumor necrosis factor alpha drugs: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: European journal of gastroenterology & hepatology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) patients undergoing antitumor necrosis factor-alpha (anti-TNF) therapy are at increased risk of developing tuberculosis (TB), making screening before anti-TNF initi...

item.title: Chest X-ray prior to biological treatment in inflammatory bowel diseases: it is not only about tuberculosis - a case report and literature review.
item.description: European journal of gastroenterology & hepatology - Biological treatments have substantially improved management of inflammatory bowel disease, but their profound immunosuppressive effects necessitate infection screening before initiation. Current g...

item.title: The prognostic role of magnetic resonance enterography at diagnosis in paediatric isolated ileocaecal Crohn's disease.
item.description: Journal of pediatric gastroenterology and nutrition - We aimed at identifying magnetic resonance enterography (MRE) predictors of surgical intervention and anti-tumor necrosis factor alpha (TNF-α) initiation in a paediatric cohort of patients newly di...

item.title: Diagnostic value of CT enterography combined with inflammatory indicators in active Crohn's disease.

item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - In patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), Clostridioides difficile infection (CDI) presents with clinical features indistinguishable from the underlying disease. Sequential polymerase chai...

item.title: Exposure to per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances and inflammatory bowel disease: review and meta-analysis.
item.description: Journal of exposure science & environmental epidemiology - Review - Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) are stable chemicals that are well preserved in the environment and persist in the human body. PFAS have been associated with inflammatory bowel disease (...

item.title: Risk factors for immune checkpoint inhibitor colitis: a retrospective multi-center cohort study using electronic health records.
item.description: Cancer immunology, immunotherapy : CII - Multicenter study - Immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICIs) are effective for many cancers but often cause immune-related adverse events, particularly gastrointestinal (GI) complications such as colitis. Identifying risk ...

item.title: Distinct clinicopathological features of IgA nephropathy associated with Crohn's disease: comparison with ulcerative colitis and non-IBD IgA nephropathy.
item.description: Clinical and experimental nephrology - IgA nephropathy (IgAN) is increasingly recognized as a complication of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), particularly Crohn's disease (CD). Recent studies suggest that mucosal immune dysregulation ...

item.title: Neuro-immune interactions underlying autoimmune diseases: insights from brain imaging data.
item.description: Molecular brain - Autoimmune diseases (AIDs) result from intricate interactions among genetic, environmental and immune factors, with emerging evidence underscoring the role of the central nervous system (CNS) in th...

item.title: Defining Moderate Disease Activity and Severity in Crohn's Disease and Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Journal of clinical gastroenterology - This research aimed to review the literature for definitions of moderate Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC). Real-world evidence suggests that biologics may achieve better outcomes in...
